findings.title: Strict spec validation exits before the documented shell fallback can run
evidence: command: bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <target-spec> --strict -> exit 1 with no diagnostics
findings.impact: The completion verification gate can fail silently in a source-only checkout, so agents cannot distinguish a real spec error from an orchestrator bootstrap miss.
findings.fix: Call `run_node_orchestrator` inside a conditional and fall through to shell validators when it returns 1; preserve hard failures from the orchestrator separately.

findings.title: Level 1 documentation requires implementation-summary.md, but the file rule treats it as optional until completion
findings.impact: Spec packets can validate under one enforcement path while missing a file that the global framework and template structure call mandatory.
findings.fix: Make the Level 1 file rule and template structure agree, or explicitly amend the framework table to say implementation-summary.md is conditional.

findings.title: Comment hygiene is documented as two non-bypassable gates, but the authorized owner flow can bypass both
findings.impact: A direct owner push to main can skip the PR-only workflow and local hooks, so the constitutional claim overstates the effective enforcement surface.
findings.fix: Either enforce comment hygiene on push/main CI as well, or soften the constitutional rule to name the documented bypass cases and required audit trail.

findings.title: The review target packet is Level 1 but is missing required docs and metadata
evidence: missing files observed: plan.md, tasks.md, implementation-summary.md, description.json, graph-metadata.json
findings.impact: The target is harder to resume, search, and validate because the packet-level metadata and continuity files are absent.
findings.fix: Create the Level 1 docs and mandatory metadata for this packet, or explicitly convert it into a documented fan-out-only review control artifact with its own validation rule.

findings.title: sk-code authoring checklist assets fail sk-doc asset validation
findings.impact: The resources sk-code tells authors to load can fail the documentation validator that governs asset files.
findings.fix: Rename or add an `Overview` section in the affected checklist assets, or add a more specific sk-code checklist document type to sk-doc's rules.

findings.title: Python implementations with .sh names trigger sk-code shell verifier warnings
findings.impact: `verify_alignment_drift.py --root .opencode/skills/sk-code` passes only because warnings are non-fatal; `--fail-on-warn` turns the current sk-code tree red.
findings.fix: Rename the Python scripts to `.py` and update callers, or teach the verifier to classify by shebang before extension.
